projects.descricao: A modern and responsive website developed in HTML, CSS with Tailwind and JavaScript with Swiper and I18Next, to present my projects, skills and professional trajectory in a dynamic and attractive way. The design combines vibrant colors with a clear and uncluttered structure, providing a unique and straightforward visual experience. Designed to highlight my work, my journey and about me, with the option of translation and change of theme. (You're in it at the moment).

projects.descricao:
A complete network calculator for IPv4 and IPv6, designed to facilitate studies, network configuration, and address analysis.

The project performs automatic calculations of:

IPv4, IPv6, Network Masks, CIDR, Binary Conversion, Broadcast, Hosts, Network ID, Subnets, IPv6 Compression (RFC 5952), Full Network Information

Demonstration of Using the Site in IPv4 Creation

projects.descricao: I developed a front-end web application that allows users to encrypt and decrypt text messages. Using HTML, CSS, and pure JavaScript, I created a responsive interface and implemented string manipulation logic for the coding functions, as well as user interaction functionalities such as copying the result to the clipboard.

projects.descricao: I developed a Java console application that performs currency conversions with real-time exchange rates. The project demonstrates competence in the consumption of REST APIs using the native Java HTTP client, in addition to the parsing of JSON responses with the Gson library. The application has an interactive interface that allows the user to select the source and target currencies to get the converted amount instantly.

projects.descricao:
Development of an intelligent lighting system with Arduino Uno, capable of interpreting analog audio signals via P2/P3 input (3.5mm) and converting sound frequencies into RGB light effects. The project uses a proprietary algorithm in Arduino for analysis and mapping of musical frequencies (bass, mid and treble) in specific colors, controlling RGB LED strips in real time.

Connectivity is via Bluetooth (HC-05) and Wi-Fi (ESP8266), allowing the user to manually control colors, modes, and intensities through an Android app developed in the MIT App Inventor. The system offers automatic modes reactive to music and manual modes of customization.

The solution is compact, portable and aimed at environments such as parties, studios and home use, with a focus on a visual experience synchronized with sound and easy integration by app.

projects.titulo: Arduino Voice Control System with Wi-Fi and Bluetooth
projects.descricao: The project consists of an Arduino-based automation system integrated with a Wi-Fi shield and Bluetooth module, controlled by an application developed in the MIT App Inventor. The app uses voice commands to send instructions to the Arduino, allowing the activation and control of devices such as mini servo motors and LEDs. The hybrid communication architecture (Wi-Fi and Bluetooth) ensures greater flexibility and range, enabling both local and remote control. The system demonstrates practical applications in home automation, prototyping, and IoT teaching, combining voice interaction and wireless connectivity in a low-cost, easy-to-replicate environment.

media.descricao: Demonstration of Prototype assembly on thinkercad
Demonstration of the Use of the APP

projects.descricao: REST API developed with Spring Boot as part of the Digital Innovation One (DIO) bootcamp, aimed at managing people in a registration system. The project follows good architectural practices and uses tools such as JPA, Hibernate and H2 Database for data persistence. Includes testing with JUnit and integration with Swagger for automatic API documentation.

media.descricao: Spring boot loading screen with folder view and organization

projects.descricao: I developed a complete REST API that simulates a discussion forum, using the Spring ecosystem (Boot, Security, Data JPA). The project includes a stateless authentication system based on JWT tokens, CRUD operations for topic and response management, and data validations to ensure the robustness and security of the application, following the best practices of back-end development.

media.descricao: Code Organization Demo and its DTOs

projects.descricao: I developed an interactive console application in Java and Spring Boot that works as a book catalog. The application consumes the Gutendex public API to fetch information about books and authors, deserializes JSON responses to Java objects, and uses Spring Data JPA to persist the data in a PostgreSQL database. The interactive menu allows the user to perform complex searches and queries on the stored data.

media.descricao: Code Organization Demo and its organization

projects.descricao: Project developed at the Digital Innovation One (DIO) bootcamp, focusing on applying UML modeling concepts and Object-Oriented Programming in Java. The proposal is to simulate basic functionalities of an iPhone — such as calls, music playback and internet browsing — through well-structured interfaces and classes.

media.descricao: UML Demo in Preview Model

projects.descricao: Application developed in MIT App Inventor to generate fast and reliable random number draws, ideal for use in events, games or unbiased decisions. It has a simple and intuitive interface, allowing the user to easily set the desired numerical range and execute the draw with just one touch. The system guarantees unpredictable results, offering practicality and efficiency in any situation that requires random selection.

media.descricao: Running the App for Demo

projects.descricao: Practical implementation of the main design patterns (Design Patterns) of the GoF (Gang of Four) catalog using Java. Developed as part of the Digital Innovation One (DIO) bootcamp, the project explores Creational, Structural, and Behavioral patterns, demonstrating their application in the context of object-oriented development.

media.descricao: Spring boot loading screen with folder view and organization

projects.descricao: Project developed during the bootcamp of Digital Innovation One (DIO), focusing on the fundamentals and practice of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) in Java. The application demonstrates concepts such as classes, abstraction, encapsulation, inheritance and polymorphism, through a simple and well-structured modeling.

media.descricao: Spring boot loading screen with folder view and organization

projects.descricao: Practical project of a digital banking system developed in Java, as part of the Digital Innovation One (DIO) bootcamp. iBank simulates essential functionalities of a bank, such as account opening, deposits, withdrawals and transfers, with a focus on the application of the pillars of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP).

media.descricao: Standardized CMD-style Menu view

projects.descricao: I developed a program to validate input parameters and control the flow of execution with loops and conditionals. Highlight for the creation of a custom exception for error handling.

media.descricao: Menu and Output Demo

projects.descricao: I created a Java console application that simulates the process of opening a bank account. The program collects user data (agency, account, name) via terminal, processes this information, and displays a formatted confirmation message. This project strengthened my skills in handling data input and output and using primitive data types in Java.
